violations.claim: VIOLATION: uninstall()'s precondition is that the hook is currently installed.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 465
; VIOLATION: uninstall()'s precondition is that the hook is currently installed.
; runHook never calls isInstalled() in this branch before invoking uninstall().
; Strategy: model the code's actual transitions + violation condition → expect sat.

(declare-const flag_uninstall         Int)   ; 1 iff flag present (branch is active)
(declare-const flag_remove            Int)
(declare-const hook_currently_installed Int) ; 1 iff hook file exists on disk
(declare-const is_installed_checked   Int)   ; 1 iff isInstalled() called before uninstall()

; Code transition: uninstall branch is active
(assert (or (= flag_uninstall 1) (= flag_remove 1)))

; Code transition: the uninstall arm contains no call to isInstalled()
;   (the only call to isInstalled() in the function is inside the --status arm)
(assert (= is_installed_checked 0))

; Code transition: uninstall() is called unconditionally — it executes regardless of hook state
; (hook_currently_installed is never read before the call)

; Violation condition: uninstall() invoked when hook is absent
(assert (= hook_currently_installed 0))

(check-sat)

; Code transition: the uninstall arm contains no call to isInstalled()

(assert (= is_installed_checked 0))

; Code transition: uninstall() is called unconditionally — it executes regardless of hook state
; (hook_currently_installed is never read before the call)

(assert (= hook_currently_installed 0))

(check-sat)
